author: @thinkingoutsidethegrid5625
text:
EARTH WALL BATTERY... possible?

Rob, your channel is the only one I check daily to see if you have posted anything new!  I've learned so much from your videos and can't express how grateful I am for them.  I've stumbled across something very exciting in my opinion and I'm hoping I could get your quick thoughts.  Here is a link to one of my videos that I posted.  You don't even have to watch the video, but if you could read the description and tell me if you think it is possible or not.  Thank you!
https://youtu.be/YgSklB4w43E

author: @bryanchannell7715
text: Thanks again

author: @stevea5407
text:
Hi Both , I am a twizy owner and I am eagerly following  your twizy experiments. What I would like to know is can I buy or build ( with a kit or plans from you for instance)your twizy battery in the near future. I think you would have a lot of takers. Good Luck .
Steve

author: @aga5897
text:
Where are you all at right now ?
I get the feeling that life's been a bit shitty recently, especially with the Indi-nono nonsense.
"Ortorea Screenname" (see comments to this vid) suggests you get Fans to 3D print the blade parts.
I got 2x 3D printers and would happily print and post you some for free, as i suspect many others would.
You have a lot of Fans with a huge amount of resources, collectively.
Make use of your resources.
I think it OK to say that we'd all prefer to be involved, if even just a small way.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Cheers mate

author: @zylascope
text: Thanks :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: No problem.....

author: @dharmabodhy
text: Fantastic Patty!  Please Robert let always her present your videos. I was deciding to cancel from this group. Even though topics are very interesting it is too difficult for me to understand  your pronunciation.  Instead I understood every single word of Patty. And is decisely prettier than you. :-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: It's probably the American in her.....

replies.author: @dharmabodhy
replies.text: "We find .... "  you use to talk in Royal We? Anyway it depends whether the targeted audience is proudly UK-bounded  or wider :-)

replies.author: @craigglewis
replies.text: I use "We" all the time. It so I don't look like I'm the Only one working. There is a Team and that means "WE" more than just oneself. It's a Lonely world if you leave and are by yourself. Understanding or not understanding is a Personal Choice. One can Learn to listen more intently, and be open to others diversity, yes? Is your first language English? If not, this may be why you find it hard to hear English.

replies.author: @dharmabodhy
replies.text: A ok now i see it is a way to face loneliness! When u go to the Pub alone and ask “we want a beer” and the bartender asks "how many?”  what is the average number you answer usually? And in that case you drink all those beers alone? Anyway my kind comment was not to you all but to Robert so it seems to me that you and your team  should go back to the  beers and butt out.

replies.author: @craigglewis
replies.text: I sent a reply on my cell and I don't see it. Oh well, maybe best this way. lol PS I don't visit the pub and I "we want a beer” (Not proper English, yet I see how you find it difficult to hear Robert)  I will use next time I see a pretty bartender. Great way to start a conversation when she ask "Who is we" If you and I darling ;) lol Thanks for the pointer ;)

author: @azhar2626
text: I really appreciate your hard work and guiding other people how to make  blades and all other related stuff. I am really mentally upset you are not making power blade battery. I understand all the situation but you have to really not give up making power blade battery. God will help us from unknown source to complete the power blade battery project. God bless you

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Not giving up mate - just delayed to get things moving....

replies.author: @mjerez6029
replies.text: Don't agree at all, what it is important here is what is inside not the case, I think this is the best move with the resources they have at the moment.

author: @mSparks43
text: Lovely!! Great solution to a tough problem, Might I suggest a single direct comparison with a lead acid battery and a patent on its innards is all you need to demonstrate the value of what you have been doing and solve your financial concerns. A patented, low cost of manufacture (your ink mass produced) high energy density, low toxicity and most importantly low weight in a car battery form factor and you can take that to any battery manufacturer and demand your terms. Lead acid is still basically the standard in most use cases because for all its disadvantages, its cheap to manufacture (doesn't use rare minerals) at scale.

replies.author: @mSparks43
replies.text: I'd love to point you in the direction of a company that could help with the plastics, but I just looked and it seems all the companies I dealt with in the early 2000's have gone bust :( The word for that is probably "China"....

author: @aerotro
text: Is the Blade 3D printable ? why not ask volunteers to donate a fraction of their 3D Printer time to print some and send them in to Rob ? I am sure most of us would be willing to chip in a little bit of 3D printing time I would ;)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Cheers mate

replies.author: @JehuMcSpooran
replies.text: It would be better to have them injection molded rather than 3D printed as 3D prints can be quite leaky.

replies.author: @aerotro
replies.text: This is quite true, however their are simple ways to seal 3D prints if printed in ABS an acetone gas bath, or painting acetone onto the surface, or spraying them with paint are all methods of sealing them :) PLA is not so friendly only spray painting them can seal PLA or brush painting.

author: @ericlinz4626
text: It's always a good idea to create a kind of "Version 1.0" battery first and get it out there and functioning before you go all the way into a manufactured product.  Proving the chemistry is the first goal...  then make it pretty.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think I've shown quite well that the chemistry works - its now getting it into production!

author: @truetech4158
text:
People local to the area would stand to learn a lot by checking out their structured seminars. I can't swim that far from way over here, though just remain inspired to think a lot about concepts on the topic of harvesting even a slice of a universes worth of useful electrons. 

Humbling stats have been recorded by The Financial Times that over 12 million people are sadly removed from life as the direct result of pollution, mostly being enabled by the greed of those with oily futures portfolios. 

It's vital that we as a species focus on evolving past that in a most timely manner. This has to be super affordable and made from renewables in design to gain best traction. A universally adaptive set of standards leading to a reduction of proprietary restrictions should be in order.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thanks for the post mate.

author: @TheBaconWizard
text:
Can Rob come to my house, cook dinner, tell me how to make a world-busting battery product, fund it, do the work and give me the money? Thanks :D
In all seriousness, what is the blade's housing made-of? It looks like Perspex. If so, I have access to a laser-cutter, would that help?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thanks - we do have a laser cutter which is how we have made the prototypes, but it just all takes too long!

replies.author: @jonathanshahariw2908
replies.text: Just wondering where you can purchase ths 12v empty  battery cases.... I have been trying to find them.

replies.author: @RWBHere
replies.text: Jonathan, if all else fails, and you only want a couple, then why not ask neighbours for their old battery, or go to the scrapyard; empty the cases and have the cell materials recycled responsibly? If you want more than a couple of cases, you could approach a battery manufacturer and ask them to sell you a few cases. They might cost more, but at least you will have them.

replies.author: @jonathanshahariw2908
replies.text:
Ive found trying to take apart a sealed battery usually ends up with a case I cant reseal with new components...  If theres a way to get them apart and remove the plates without damaging the case I havent found it yet.

I have found cases out of china but with a minimum order of 2000 I just cant justify that for testing
